title: Mountain Time Zone

infobox.value: of [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America") keeps time by subtracting seven hours from [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time") (UTC) when standard time ([UTC−07:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9207:00 "UTC−07:00")) is in effect, and by subtracting six hours during [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") ([UTC−06:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9206:00 "UTC−06:00")).

(**MT**). Specifically, it is **Mountain Standard Time** (**MST**) when observing standard time, and **Mountain Daylight Time** (**MDT**) when observing daylight saving time. The term refers to the [Rocky Mountains](/wiki/Rocky_Mountains "Rocky Mountains"), which range from [British Columbia](/wiki/British_Columbia "British Columbia") to [New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico"). In Mexico, this time zone is known as the *tiempo de la montaña* or *zona Pacífico* ('Pacific Zone'). In the United States, the Mountain Time Zone is to the east of the [Pacific Time Zone](/wiki/Pacific_Time_Zone "Pacific Time Zone"); in it and Canada, it is to the west of the [Central Time Zone](/wiki/Central_Time_Zone "Central Time Zone").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Time zone of North America

"Mountain Time" redirects here. For other uses, see [Mountain Time (disambiguation)](/wiki/Mountain_Time_(disambiguation) "Mountain Time (disambiguation)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **may contain [original research](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research "Wikipedia:No original research")**. Please [improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=edit) by [verifying](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") the claims made and adding [inline citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#Inline_citations "Wikipedia:Citing sources"). Statements consisting only of original research should be removed. *(March 2026)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Mountain Time Zone | |
| --- | --- |
| [Time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") | |
| Mountain Time Zone Shown offsets are Standard | |
| UTC offset | |
| MST | [UTC−07:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9207:00 "UTC−07:00") |
| MDT | [UTC−06:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9206:00 "UTC−06:00") |
| Current time | |
| 00:17, March 27, 2026 MST [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=purge)] 01:17, March 27, 2026 MDT [[refresh](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=purge)] | |
| Observance of [DST](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") | |

The **Mountain Time Zone** of [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America") keeps time by subtracting seven hours from [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time") (UTC) when standard time ([UTC−07:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9207:00 "UTC−07:00")) is in effect, and by subtracting six hours during [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") ([UTC−06:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9206:00 "UTC−06:00")).

In Canada, Mexico, and the United States, this time zone is generically called **Mountain Time** (**MT**). Specifically, it is **Mountain Standard Time** (**MST**) when observing standard time, and **Mountain Daylight Time** (**MDT**) when observing daylight saving time. The term refers to the [Rocky Mountains](/wiki/Rocky_Mountains "Rocky Mountains"), which range from [British Columbia](/wiki/British_Columbia "British Columbia") to [New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico"). In Mexico, this time zone is known as the *tiempo de la montaña* or *zona Pacífico* ('Pacific Zone'). In the United States, the Mountain Time Zone is to the east of the [Pacific Time Zone](/wiki/Pacific_Time_Zone "Pacific Time Zone"); in it and Canada, it is to the west of the [Central Time Zone](/wiki/Central_Time_Zone "Central Time Zone").

In some areas, starting in 2007, the local time changes from MST to MDT at 2 am MST to 3 am MDT on the second Sunday in March and returns at 2 am MDT to 1 am MST on the first Sunday in November.

Most of Mexico, and most of [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona") in the United States, do not observe daylight saving time (DST), and during the spring, summer, and autumn months they are on the same time as [Pacific Daylight Time](/wiki/Pacific_Daylight_Time "Pacific Daylight Time").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Navajo Nation](/wiki/Navajo_Nation "Navajo Nation") is the only part of Arizona that observes DST. Its territory lies mostly within Arizona but extends into [Utah](/wiki/Utah "Utah") and [New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico") (both observe DST statewide). Meanwhile, the [Hopi Reservation](/wiki/Hopi_Reservation "Hopi Reservation"), despite being completely surrounded by the Navajo Nation, does not observe DST, and neither do some Arizona state offices located within the Navajo Nation.

The largest city in the Mountain Time Zone is [Phoenix, Arizona](/wiki/Phoenix,_Arizona "Phoenix, Arizona"); the [Phoenix metropolitan area](/wiki/Phoenix_metropolitan_area "Phoenix metropolitan area") is the largest metropolitan area in the zone. The largest city that observes daylight saving time is [Juárez, Chihuahua](/wiki/Ju%C3%A1rez,_Chihuahua "Juárez, Chihuahua"), Mexico.

The clock time in this zone is based on the [mean solar time](/wiki/Mean_solar_time "Mean solar time") at the [105th meridian west](/wiki/105th_meridian_west "105th meridian west") of the [Greenwich Observatory](/wiki/Greenwich_Observatory "Greenwich Observatory"). In the United States, the exact specification for the location of [time zones](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") and the dividing lines between zones is set forth in the [Code of Federal Regulations](/wiki/Code_of_Federal_Regulations "Code of Federal Regulations") at 49 CFR 71.[[a]](#cite_note-name-5)

## Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Canada")]

Main article: [Time in Canada](/wiki/Time_in_Canada "Time in Canada")

Two provinces and two territories are fully contained in the Mountain Time Zone:

* [Alberta](/wiki/Alberta "Alberta")
* [British Columbia](/wiki/British_Columbia "British Columbia") (no DST in most of province)
* [Northwest Territories](/wiki/Northwest_Territories "Northwest Territories")
* [Yukon](/wiki/Yukon "Yukon") (no DST)

On September 24, 2020, Yukon switched to the Mountain Standard Time year-round. Therefore, clocks in Yukon and Alberta are the same in the winter, and Alberta is one hour ahead in summer. Previously, the territory had used the Pacific Time Zone with daylight saving time: UTC−8 in winter and UTC−7 in summer.[[5]](#cite_note-6)

On March 8, 2026, most of British Columbia in the Pacific Time Zone adopted UTC−7 year-round but continue to call their time zone "Pacific Time". Southeastern parts of the province (near the Alberta border) continue to observe daylight saving time: UTC−6 in the summer and UTC−7 in the winter.[[6]](#cite_note-BC_permanent_DST-7)

One territory and one province are split between the Mountain Time Zone and the [Central Time Zone](/wiki/Central_Time_Zone "Central Time Zone"):

* [Nunavut](/wiki/Nunavut "Nunavut") – [Kitikmeot Region](/wiki/Kitikmeot_Region "Kitikmeot Region")
* [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan") – [Lloydminster](/wiki/Lloydminster "Lloydminster") and vicinity

## Mexico

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mexico")]

Main article: [Time in Mexico](/wiki/Time_in_Mexico "Time in Mexico")

As of October 30, 2022, Mexico abandoned [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_Mexico "Daylight saving time in Mexico"), with certain exceptions.
The following [states](/wiki/Mexican_states "Mexican states") have an offset equal to Mountain Standard Time:

* [Baja California Sur](/wiki/Baja_California_Sur "Baja California Sur")
* [Nayarit](/wiki/Nayarit "Nayarit"): except for the [Bahía de Banderas](/wiki/Bah%C3%ADa_de_Banderas "Bahía de Banderas") municipality, which uses the Central Time Zone.
* [Sonora](/wiki/Sonora "Sonora")
* [Sinaloa](/wiki/Sinaloa "Sinaloa")
* [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)"): Observes Daylight Savings Time, only in northwestern border municipalities ([Janos](/wiki/Janos_Municipality "Janos Municipality"), [Ascensión](/wiki/Ascensi%C3%B3n_Municipality "Ascensión Municipality"), [Juárez](/wiki/Ju%C3%A1rez_Municipality,_Chihuahua "Juárez Municipality, Chihuahua"), [Guadalupe](/wiki/Guadalupe_Municipality,_Chihuahua "Guadalupe Municipality, Chihuahua"), and [Práxedis Gilberto Guerrero](/wiki/Pr%C3%A1xedis_G._Guerrero_Municipality "Práxedis G. Guerrero Municipality"))
* [Revillagigedo Islands](/wiki/Revillagigedo_Islands "Revillagigedo Islands") ([Colima](/wiki/Colima "Colima")): three of the four islands have the same time as Mountain Time Zone: [Socorro Island](/wiki/Isla_Socorro "Isla Socorro"), [San Benedicto Island](/wiki/San_Benedicto_Island "San Benedicto Island") and [Roca Partida](/wiki/Roca_Partida "Roca Partida").

The following states have an offset equal to Mountain Daylight Time:

* [Aguascalientes](/wiki/Aguascalientes "Aguascalientes")
* [Campeche](/wiki/Campeche "Campeche")
* [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua_(state) "Chihuahua (state)")
* [Ciudad de México](/wiki/Ciudad_de_M%C3%A9xico "Ciudad de México")
* [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila")
* [Colima](/wiki/Colima "Colima"), except all of the [Revillagigedo Islands](/wiki/Revillagigedo_Islands "Revillagigedo Islands").
* [Chiapas](/wiki/Chiapas "Chiapas")
* [Durango](/wiki/Durango "Durango")
* [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato")
* [Guerrero](/wiki/State_of_Guerrero "State of Guerrero")
* [Hidalgo](/wiki/Estado_de_Hidalgo "Estado de Hidalgo")
* [Jalisco](/wiki/Jalisco "Jalisco")
* [México](/wiki/State_of_M%C3%A9xico "State of México")
* [Michoacán](/wiki/Michoac%C3%A1n "Michoacán")
* [Morelos](/wiki/Morelos "Morelos")
* [Nuevo León](/wiki/Nuevo_Le%C3%B3n "Nuevo León")
* [Oaxaca](/wiki/Oaxaca "Oaxaca")
* [Puebla](/wiki/Puebla "Puebla")
* [Querétaro](/wiki/Quer%C3%A9taro "Querétaro")
* [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD "San Luis Potosí")
* [Tabasco](/wiki/Tabasco "Tabasco")
* [Tamaulipas](/wiki/Tamaulipas "Tamaulipas")
* [Tlaxcala](/wiki/Tlaxcala "Tlaxcala")
* [Veracruz](/wiki/State_of_Veracruz_de_Ignacio_de_la_Llave "State of Veracruz de Ignacio de la Llave")
* [Yucatán](/wiki/Yucat%C3%A1n "Yucatán")
* [Zacatecas](/wiki/Zacatecas "Zacatecas")
* Municipality of [Bahía de Banderas](/wiki/Bah%C3%ADa_de_Banderas "Bahía de Banderas") in [Nayarit](/wiki/Nayarit "Nayarit")

## United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: United States")]

Main article: [Time in the United States](/wiki/Time_in_the_United_States "Time in the United States")

Six states are fully contained in the Mountain Time Zone:

* [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado")
* [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana")
* [New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico")
* [Utah](/wiki/Utah "Utah")
* [Wyoming](/wiki/Wyoming "Wyoming")
* [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona") (does not observe daylight saving time except for [Navajo Nation](/wiki/Navajo_Nation "Navajo Nation"))

Three states are split between the Mountain Time Zone and the [Pacific Time Zone](/wiki/Pacific_Time_Zone "Pacific Time Zone"). The following locations observe Mountain Time:

* [Idaho](/wiki/Idaho "Idaho"): [Southern Idaho](/wiki/Southern_Idaho "Southern Idaho") and the portion of [Idaho County](/wiki/Idaho_County,_Idaho "Idaho County, Idaho") south of the [Salmon River](/wiki/Salmon_River_(Idaho) "Salmon River (Idaho)")
* [Oregon](/wiki/Oregon "Oregon"): the majority of [Malheur County](/wiki/Malheur_County,_Oregon "Malheur County, Oregon")
* [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada"): [West Wendover](/wiki/West_Wendover,_Nevada "West Wendover, Nevada")
  + The communities of [Jackpot](/wiki/Jackpot,_Nevada "Jackpot, Nevada"), [Jarbidge](/wiki/Jarbidge,_Nevada "Jarbidge, Nevada"), [Mountain City](/wiki/Mountain_City,_Nevada "Mountain City, Nevada"), and [Owyhee](/wiki/Owyhee,_Nevada "Owyhee, Nevada") unofficially observe Mountain Time, although they are officially within the Pacific Time Zone.[[7]](#cite_note-8)

Five states are split between the Mountain Time Zone and the [Central Time Zone](/wiki/Central_Time_Zone "Central Time Zone"). The following locations observe Mountain Time:

* [Kansas](/wiki/Kansas "Kansas"): [Sherman](/wiki/Sherman_County,_Kansas "Sherman County, Kansas"), [Wallace](/wiki/Wallace_County,_Kansas "Wallace County, Kansas"), [Greeley](/wiki/Greeley_County,_Kansas "Greeley County, Kansas") and [Hamilton](/wiki/Hamilton_County,_Kansas "Hamilton County, Kansas") counties
* [Nebraska](/wiki/Nebraska "Nebraska"): western one third
* [North Dakota](/wiki/North_Dakota "North Dakota"): the southwest corner counties ([Adams](/wiki/Adams_County,_North_Dakota "Adams County, North Dakota"), [Billings](/wiki/Billings_County,_North_Dakota "Billings County, North Dakota"), [Bowman](/wiki/Bowman_County,_North_Dakota "Bowman County, North Dakota"), [Golden Valley](/wiki/Golden_Valley_County,_North_Dakota "Golden Valley County, North Dakota"), [Grant](/wiki/Grant_County,_North_Dakota "Grant County, North Dakota"), [Hettinger](/wiki/Hettinger_County,_North_Dakota "Hettinger County, North Dakota"), [Slope](/wiki/Slope_County,_North_Dakota "Slope County, North Dakota"), [Stark](/wiki/Stark_County,_North_Dakota "Stark County, North Dakota")) observe MST. The counties of [McKenzie](/wiki/McKenzie_County,_North_Dakota "McKenzie County, North Dakota"), [Dunn](/wiki/Dunn_County,_North_Dakota "Dunn County, North Dakota"), and [Sioux](/wiki/Sioux_County,_North_Dakota "Sioux County, North Dakota") are split.
* [South Dakota](/wiki/South_Dakota "South Dakota"): western half
* [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"): [El Paso](/wiki/El_Paso_County,_Texas "El Paso County, Texas"), [Hudspeth](/wiki/Hudspeth_County,_Texas "Hudspeth County, Texas"), and northwestern [Culberson](/wiki/Culberson_County,_Texas "Culberson County, Texas") counties

## Major metropolitan areas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Major metropolitan areas")]

Alphabetical list of major cities located within the Mountain Time Zone. Cities in **bold** do not observe daylight saving time:

* [Albuquerque, New Mexico](/wiki/Albuquerque,_New_Mexico "Albuquerque, New Mexico")

* [Colorado Springs, Colorado](/wiki/Colorado_Springs,_Colorado "Colorado Springs, Colorado")
* **[Culiacán](/wiki/Culiac%C3%A1n "Culiacán"), Sinaloa**
* [Metropolitan Denver](/wiki/Denver_metropolitan_area "Denver metropolitan area")
  + [Arvada, Colorado](/wiki/Arvada,_Colorado "Arvada, Colorado")
  + [Aurora, Colorado](/wiki/Aurora,_Colorado "Aurora, Colorado")

* [Lake Havasu City, Arizona](/wiki/Lake_Havasu_City,_Arizona "Lake Havasu City, Arizona")

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mountain_Time_Zone&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-name_5-0)** The specification for the Mountain Time Zone in the United States is set forth at 49 CFR 71.8.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The boundary between Central and Mountain time zones is set forth at 49 CFR 71.7,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the boundary between Mountain and Pacific time zones is set forth at 49 CFR 71.9.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
